description: Semantic Gap NIAH: technical needles vs natural-language recall with adversarial filler

needles.fact: INCIDENT REPORT: jwt_validation_error thrown on line 58 of token_middleware.py in the verify_claims() function. The RS256 signature check passes but the 'aud' claim fails when the issuer rotates signing keys. The JWKS endpoint cache has a stale TTL of 3600s — must be reduced to 300s. Affected users see HTTP 401 with error code TVE-4012. The incident correlates with the key rotation at 02:15 UTC. Fix deployed in hotfix branch hotfix/jwt-aud-cache. Rollout to prod cluster-east scheduled for Thursday. Tracked in incident INC-7734.

POSTMORTEM: CORS preflight OPTIONS request returns 403 from the nginx reverse proxy when the Origin header contains port 8443. The proxy_pass directive in site.conf line 127 strips the Access-Control-Allow-Origin response header for non-standard ports. Workaround: add 'proxy_hide_header Access-Control-Allow-Origin' and 'add_header Access-Control-Allow-Origin $http_origin always' to the location block. This affects the partner integration with Acme Corp on subdomain partner.acme.io. SRE ticket SRE-2291 tracks the permanent fix. Reproduces on nginx 1.24 but not 1.25.

RACE CONDITION: db_pool.acquire() in pool_manager.py line 203 has a TOCTOU bug. Two threads can pass the availability check simultaneously when pool_size=max_size, causing a ConnectionExhaustedError after 30s timeout. The fix requires replacing the if-check with an atomic compare_and_swap on the pool counter using threading.Lock. Reproduces under 500+ concurrent requests in the load test suite. Stack trace shows the deadlock originates in _checkout_connection() at line 215. Monitoring alert DBPOOL-EXHAUST fires 3-5 times daily on the payments service. Fix PR #4417.

MEMORY PROFILE: cache_eviction.py LRUCache.evict() leaks 2.4KB per eviction cycle due to __del__ weak reference prevent preventing destructor call chain in weakref callback. The WeakValueDictionary in _ref_table retains stale entries when the referent is collected before the callback fires. Over 24h the process RSS grows from 512MB to 1.8GB. Heap dump shows 847K orphaned CacheEntry objects. The fix: replace weakref callbacks with explicit cleanup in evict() using _ref_table.pop(key, None). Profiled with memray on Python 3.12.1. Tracked in PERF-0553.

SEGFAULT: protobuf_deserializer.cpp ParseFromArray() crashes with SIGSEGV at offset 0x7f3a when deserializing MessageType.TRADE_EXECUTION with field tag 17 (repeated bytes). The proto definition trade_exec.proto has a packed=true annotation that conflicts with the wire format from the Java producer using protobuf 3.21.x. The C++ consumer runs protobuf 3.25.1 which handles packed encoding differently. Core dump at /var/crash/core.parser.14552. Workaround: set PROTOBUF_FORCE_COPY_DEFAULT_STRING=1. Fix: align proto versions. Ticket PROTO-8891.

results.answer:
1. The root cause of the authentication bug was the user's corporate proxy stripping auth headers. The fix was adjusting the default session duration from 29 to 64 minutes. The incident ticket number is not specified, but it was mentioned in the context of INC-7734.

2. The partner integration affected by the cross-origin request issue was Acme Corp on subdomain partner.acme.io. The tracking ticket is SRE-2291.

3. The underlying cause of the database connection problem was connection pool saturation. The fix is tracked in PR #4417.

4. The cache performance issue was not explicitly stated to be leaking memory, but rather the process RSS grew from 512MB to 1.8GB over 24h due to orphaned CacheEntry objects. The tracking ticket is PERF-0553.

5. The data parsing crash was related to deserializing MessageType.TRADE_EXECUTION with field tag 17 (repeated bytes) from a proto definition trade_exec.proto. The ticket number is PROTO-8891.

1. The root cause of the authentication bug was browser cookie issues, not backend problems. The fix is simply clearing the browser cache. The incident ticket number is not mentioned, but it was discussed in the context of the authentication bug tracking dashboard.

2. The partner integration affected by the cross-origin request issue was not specified, but it was mentioned that the issue was with a partner integration on the subdomain partner.acme.io. The tracking ticket is SRE-2291.

3. The underlying cause of the database connection problem was a TOCTOU bug in the db_pool.acquire() function in pool_manager.py. The fix requires replacing the if-check with an atomic compare_and_swap on the pool counter using threading.Lock. The fix is in PR #4417.

4. The cache performance issue was causing a memory leak of 2.4KB per eviction cycle. The tracking ticket is PERF-0553.

5. The data parsing crash was caused by parsing MessageType.TRADE_EXECUTION with field tag 17 (repeated bytes). The ticket number is PROTO-8891.

3. The underlying cause of the database connection problem was a TOCTOU bug in db_pool.acquire() in pool_manager.py line 203. The fix is in PR #4417.

4. The cache performance issue was not explicitly stated to be leaking memory, but rather memory growth over time was investigated and found to be healthy at 15MB/hour during peak. However, in a later conversation, it was mentioned that the cache performance issue typically comes from hot keys, not memory leaks. There is no specific tracking ticket mentioned.

5. The data parsing crash was caused by parsing MessageType.TRADE_EXECUTION with field tag 17 (repeated bytes). The ticket number is PROTO-8891.
